sammy-s-1-e-1-lost-david-spade-cartoon-2.desc:
The story goes that back in 2000, when "God, the Devil and Bob" failed to meet NBC's expectations, NBC cancelled all shows that were to be a part of their upcoming adult animation block. "God, the Devil and Bob" got sold off to Adult Swim, while "Sammy" aired two episodes per its contract and was permanently shelved by NBC.

the 13 episodes were uploaded to Matt's youtube channel and were finally uploaded in 2021. After being unaired for 20 years.


Uploader Note: I stumbled on this by chance on youtube, I looked up what it was and was surprised it was lost media recently recovered so I grabbed it as soon as possible. Then watched the whole series. I'd say it's not bad, the season ending kinda threw me abit but I won't spoil it. But I'd like to see if anyone would be able to upscale this. Clearly the tapes are out there and maybe one day that'll happen. It has that early 2000's feel of adult cartoon comedy. It was very enjoyable watching it for the first time a whole cartoon from that era that I just could never have seen before. Like watching a nostalgic show for the first time.

01HowToSwimInTenEasyLessons_file5.desc:
The Army-Navy Screen Magazine Number 3, 1943. (AKA "The War No. 3")
  • 01 How To Swim In 10 Easy Lessons: Soldiers swim through fire during amphibious training at Camp Gordon Johnston in Florida.
  • 02 Back Home - New York City: Shows wartime conditions in NYC, including rationing.
  • 03 Soldiers Of The Snow: Life for Soviet soldiers on the Eastern Front
  • 04 Strictly G.I. - V-Mail: How V-Mail works.
  • 05 U.S. Marine Corps - General Vandegrift Talks About Guadalcanal : USMC General Vandegrift introduces combat footage of the Guadalcanal campaign.
  • The Army-Navy Screen Magazine 03 1943: The entire newsreel

The Army-Navy Screen Magazine was produced by the U.S. Army Signal Corps Pictorial Service, under the supervision of Col. Frank Capra, who came up with the idea. It was released twice a month and shown to military troops as a 20-minute newsreel.

"By the end of the war, the Army-Navy Screen Magazine reached an enormous weekly audience of 4.2 million. It had become for American soldiers all over the world a communal experience that greatly influenced their perception of the war."

It featured short documentary films, news from back home, and short training films like Dr. Seuss' Private Snafu cartoons. I've uploaded the entire Screen Magazine and also each segment as a separate file (some editions are comprised of 1 long film, though).

Description at the National Archives: "Reel 1, Part 1, troops at Camp Johnston, Fla., abandon equipment while swimming, jump from towers, and swim under burning oil. Part 2 contrasts prewar N.Y.C. traffic and bright lights with wartime blackout conditions. Cars are stored because of the gas shortage; consumers use ration books for food. Part 3, Russian troops use sleds, eat at field kitchens, and snipe at Germans. Reel 2, Part 1 describes V-mail procedures: cutting, microfilming, transport, photographic enlargement, and processing. Part 2, Gen. Vandegrift describes the Guadalcanal campaign. Shows the landing, brief combat scenes, and Japanese prisoners."

As always, any error or typo is mine and if you enjoy my uploads or if you intend to make $ or otherwise profit from these files, please consider making a donation to a veteran's charity of your choice and/or Archive.org itself. Thanks. :)

tomgoestothemayor_file24.desc:
Tom Goes to the Mayor is an American adult animated sitcom created by Tim Heidecker and Eric Wareheim for Cartoon Network's late night programming block, Adult Swim. It premiered on November 14, 2004 and ended on September 25, 2006 with a total of 30 episodes.

Tom Goes to the Mayor began as a web cartoon on timanderic.com. It was popular enough to get a web sequel, in which David Cross guest stars. Bob Odenkirk noticed the show's potential, and began producing it for Adult Swim.

Adult Swim has described Tom Goes to the Mayor as "one of the most polarizing shows" they have ever had, indicating that viewers "either love it or hate it."

tower-prep_file11.desc:
Tower Prep is a teen science-fiction television series created for Cartoon Network by Paul Dini, who is known for previously writing and producing series in the DC Animated Universe. From October 12 to December 28, 2010, the series aired one thirteen-episode season, before being canceled by Cartoon Network.

The series centers around "Tower Prep", an isolated boarding school for teenagers with supernatural abilities. The protagonist (Ian Archer) and a group of friends (Gabe Forrest, Suki Sato, and CJ Ward) are unwilling students at the institution, with no memory of how they got there. Throughout the series, they explore the mysteries of Tower Prep in the hope of escaping and returning to their normal lives.

the-oblongs-s-01-e-01-misfit-love_file11.desc:
The Oblongs is an American adult animated sitcom created by Angus Oblong and Jace Richdale. It was Mohawk Productions' first venture into animation. The series premiered on April 1, 2001, on The WB, and was cancelled due to low ratings on May 20, leaving the last five episodes unaired.[1] The remaining episodes were later aired on Cartoon Network's late-night programming block Adult Swim in August 2002, with the series premiering on the network in production order. The series is loosely based on a series of characters introduced in a picture book entitled Creepy Susie and 13 Other Tragic Tales for Troubled Children.[2]

Warner Bros. won the bidding for the rights to turn Angus Oblong's characters into a series; Fox and ABC also made unsuccessful bids. The show was produced by Film Roman, Oblong Productions, Jobsite Productions and Mohawk Productions in association with Warner Bros. Television, and the theme song for the series was composed and performed by They Might Be Giants. This was the only animated series to date to have been produced by Bruce Helford under Mohawk.

A total of 13 episodes were produced. All thirteen episodes of The Oblongs were released on DVD on October 4, 2005.

scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.show: Scooby Doo & Scrappy Doo (1980 TV Series)
scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.desc:
Last Uploaded: January 30, 2022
Updated On: January 24, 2023



Significant changes were made to the show in its second season. Fred, Velma, and Daphne were dropped from the series and Shaggy, Scooby traveled all over the world on their own. Stories were also reduced to 7-minute segments and played at intervals with segments of mysteries but encountered geniune supernatural beings with Shaggy and Scooby did their best to escape and Scrappy did his best to splat. The tone of the stories was also changed to a typed of silly comedy, where the bad guys sometimes even talked directly to the camera. This format was used throughout the second and third seasons.

In the series' fourth and final season its format was changed again. Segments were not rotated with an unrelated canine cartoon starring Petey the Puppy as part of The Scooby & Scrappy-Doo/Puppy Hour. Stories reverted to the more traditional tone Scooby fans were used to except that, with a few exceptions, the supernatural element was eliminated from the show althougher. Shaggy, Scooby, and Scrappy worked for the Fearless Detective Agency, owned by Shaggy's uncle Fearless Shaggyfurt, on cases that involved ordinary criminals committing common crimes. Shaggy's uncle was never seen, but when he had a case, he would telephone the agency and give the three their assignment. At other times they took cases directly form visiting clients. How Shaggy and Scooby came to work for the agency may have been the biggest mystery, because they remained as cowardly as ever and were never enthusiastic about getting a case.

An additional segment was included in the fourth season that starred Scrappy-Doo and his (other) Uncle Yabba-Doo. Stories took place in the remote Western town of Tumbleweed, where Scrappy and Yabba assisted the town deputy, Dusty, in maintaing law and order.

As was customary with the Scooby-Doo cartoons, before long the show was reformatted and presented under a different title in order to keep things fresh. In 1983 a new series was produced called The New Scooby & Scrappy-Doo Show. Scrappy-Doo was also later featured in The New Scooby-Doo Mysteries (1984) and The 13 Ghosts of Scooby-Doo (1985). Other shows with a and Shaggy and Scooby-Doo Get a Clue! (2005).

kihachiro-kawamoto-film-works_file5.desc:
Born in 1925, from an early age Kihachirō Kawamoto was captivated by the art of doll and puppet making. After seeing the works of maestro Czech animator Jiří Trnka, he first became interested in stop motion puppet animation and during the 50s began working alongside Japan's first stop motion animator, the legendary Tadahito Mochinaga. In 1958, he co-founded Shiba Productions to make commercial animation for television, but it was not until 1963, when he traveled to Prague to study puppet animation under Jiří Trnka for a year, that his puppets truly began to take on a life of their own. Trnka encouraged Kawamoto to draw on his own country's rich cultural heritage in his work, and so Kawamoto returned from Czechoslovakia to make a series of highly individual, independently-produced artistic short works, beginning with Breaking of Branches is Forbidden (Hana-Ori) in 1968. Heavily influence by the traditional aesthetics of Noh, Bunraku doll theatre and Kabuki, since the 70s his haunting puppet animations such as The Demon (Oni, 1972), Dōjōji Temple (Dōjōji, 1976) and House of Flame (Kataku, 1979) have won numerous prizes internationally. He has also produced cut-out (kirigami) animations such as The Trip (Tabi, 1973) and A Poet's Life (Shijin no Shogai, 1974). In 1990 he returned to Trnka's studios in Prague to make Briar Rose, or The Sleeping Beauty. A selection of Kihachiro Kawamoto's shorts are currently available on DVD from Geneon: Kihachiro Kawamoto Film Works.

Breaking of Branches is Forbidden (花折り, Hana-Ori?, 1968, 14 min.)
Anthropo-Cynical Farce (Farce anthropo-cynique, 1970, 8 min., from a story by Riichi Yokomitsu)
The Demon (鬼, Oni?, 1972, 8 min.)
Travel (旅, Tabi?, 1973, 12 min.)
A Poet's Life (詩人の生涯, Shijin no Shōgai?, 1974, 19 min., from a story by Kōbō Abe)
Dōjōji (道成寺, 1976, 19 min., from the Nō play of the same name?)
House of Flame (火宅, Kataku?, 1979, 19 min.)
Self-Portrait (セルフポートレート, 1988, 1 min.?)
To Shoot without Shooting (不射之射, Fusha no Sha?, 1988, 25 min.)
Briar-Rose or The Sleeping Beauty (いばら姫またはねむり姫, Ibara-Hime matawa Nemuri-Hime?, 1990, 22 min., from a story by Kyōko Kishida)

TheLostTapesShortFilmMedley2004_file0.show: The Lost Tapes Short Film Medley ( 2004)
TheLostTapesShortFilmMedley2004_file0.episode: The Lost Tapes - Short Film Medley (2004).mp4
TheLostTapesShortFilmMedley2004_file0.desc:
Video uploaded to YouTube by user: “PioneersProductions” (Randy Robert Stair)


Original description:


"EGS (Ember's Ghost Squad") - http://www.youtube.com/EGSworld


"The Lost Tapes" are a blast from the past...Tapes that were once collecting dust on my shelves are now being released on YouTube for the world to see. These DV tapes contain cringey footage from my years before YouTube. The tapes mostly range from 2004-2005. Some tapes don't display dates so it's impossible to pinpoint the exact years. These are being released not only to show you how I was back when I was 11/12/13, but to show where it all led to. The tapes can range anywhere from 30 seconds to 2 hours. They're all raw and unedited recordings (abridged for YouTube). I doubt I'll ever release all of them in their entirety unless by demand because they go on forever.


Picture yourself just pretending you're making a film, only you're recording 95% of everything that's happening all the time without stopping the camera for 50% of the video. It's a LOT of footage. There were never any scripts, very little direction, and all improvised. Typically what would happen is we'd cut and I or Matt would say "this is where it should go", and we'd just record whatever happened in the moment. We never thought these would ever be seen by anyone but ourselves, so it's pretty surreal knowing the entire world is now able to see this stuff. I haven't spoken to Matt since late 9th grade (early 2008), nor seen him since before graduation (2011). I haven't even been able to find him on any social media, so it bums me out not being able to show him this stuff. I've had so many dreams lately where I'd be hanging out with him...even though I haven't physically seen him in 5 1/2 years - 6 years...I don't miss him much but it triggered the idea to finally try and transfer these old tapes to digital to release to the world. We were best friends from 1st - 9th grade; a long time. However things changed in 9th grade when he got a girlfriend. I was still the baby faced kid whereas he was much more mature than me and hit puberty years before I even sniffed it.


After we went separate ways (which was never officially decided; we literally just stopped talking to each other in early 2008), I started "PioneersProductions", only around 3 months after the fact; and also started to become good friends with James (Freakshow180). Matt connected well with me in our earlier years of life but as things started changing in high school I just didn't fit that social zone he was in anymore. I also felt like he secretly hated me for years and couldn't tell if he purposely tried to hang out with me to be nice. It still sticks with me to this day but I just brush it off since we were really just pre-teens at that time; but being we hung out constantly for 8 1/2 years it'd be VERY hard to believe he faked his way through that for that long. In the end I think it was just a friend zone thing (not "gay" friendship or anything haha what I mean is the friends he hung out with besides me were much more socially outgoing and vocal, whereas I was an isolated kid who only hung out with Matt). So by time we transitioned into high school I probably just wasn't the kind of person he wanted to hang around with anymore. I don't know but I'm glad we took different paths because of where it led me to. I met James in 6th grade "Reading" class but we didn't become good friends until the end of 9th grade (just before me and Matt parted ways).


It might be hard to believe but if you REALLY analyze these tapes it almost seems like we were more than friends XD But in reality I'm not gay, nor do I think he is...but I couldn't tell you that hahaha There's no hidden meaning in any of this, it was really just one of those things where you had a kid who was isolated, quiet, and independent, who found a kid who he just connected and bonded well with. Unfortunately not all friendships last forever. One thing's for sure though, when we got the video camera out we made some pretty unusual and hilarious stuff.

Home Movies is an American animated sitcom created by Brendon Small and Loren Bouchard. The show centers on an eight-year-old aspiring filmmaker, also named Brendon Small, who makes homemade film productions in his spare time with his friends Melissa Robbins and Jason Penopolis. He lives with his divorced mother Paula and his adopted baby sister Josie. He develops a skewed father-son-like relationship with his alcoholic, short-tempered soccer coach, John McGuirk.

The show was produced by Tom Snyder Productions (now known as Soup2Nuts), who were previously famous for their work on the 1995 Comedy Central animated series Dr. Katz, Professional Therapist. Home Movies premiered on UPN on April 26, 1999, and ran for four seasons. The show was later picked up by Adult Swim, where it aired its final season in 2004.

Home Movies is known for its improvisational animation style, which gives the show a loose and naturalistic feel. The show's dialogue is often improvised by the actors, and the animation is done in a simple, hand-drawn style. This gives the show a unique look and feel that has helped it to develop a cult following.

The show's creators, Brendon Small and Loren Bouchard, have both gone on to have successful careers in animation. Small is the creator of the Adult Swim animated series Metalocalypse, and Bouchard is the creator of the Fox animated series Bob's Burgers.

Home Movies is a funny, heartwarming, and insightful look at the life of an aspiring filmmaker. The show is full of memorable characters, quotable lines, and hilarious situations. If you're a fan of animation, comedy, or coming-of-age stories, then you should definitely check out Home Movies.

tower-prep_file2.desc:
Tower Prep is a teen science-fiction television series created for Cartoon Network by Paul Dini, who is known for previously writing and producing series in the DC Animated Universe. From October 12 to December 28, 2010, the series aired one thirteen-episode season, before being canceled by Cartoon Network.

The series centers around "Tower Prep", an isolated boarding school for teenagers with supernatural abilities. The protagonist (Ian Archer) and a group of friends (Gabe Forrest, Suki Sato, and CJ Ward) are unwilling students at the institution, with no memory of how they got there. Throughout the series, they explore the mysteries of Tower Prep in the hope of escaping and returning to their normal lives.

DragonBallDaimaAdultSwimBrasil_file10.desc:
Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

Not long after making it official at the moonpool, Mistystar loses her last life mid argument in front of the whole clan, Frostpaw included. Reedwhisker, Riverclan’s deputy, doesn’t return from patrol that night and is missing for a while after. After receiving a vision during another trip to the moonpool, Frostpaw leads a patrol to look for Reedwhisker. She ends up finding him dead at the bottom of a ravine. Being the only cat in the clan who can communicate with Starclan anymore, she is thrust into somewhat of a leadership position and returns to the moonpool for a sign. She interprets it to mean Curlfeather to be the next leader. On the way to receive Curlfeather’s nine lives, however, a pack of dogs appears. The dogs catch and maul Curlfeather as Frostpaw barely escapes. Her last words are a warning to Frostpaw, to trust no cat. At the end of the book Frostpaw returns to the medicine den, traumatized, while the other cats discuss what happens next.

mesqtx-Mesquite_police_and_fire_associat.show: Mesquite police and fire associations donate to Mesquite NAACP mental health scholarship
mesqtx-Mesquite_police_and_fire_associat.desc:
The Mesquite Police Association and the Mesquite Firefighters Association-Local 1518 teamed up to donate $1,000 towards the Mesquite NAACP’s Mental Health Scholarship Fund that offers funding to students in the Mesquite Independent School District who want to pursue a degree towards a mental health profession.

Mesquite Mayor Bruce Archer said, “We are so happy to take this next step as one part of the City of Mesquite’s comprehensive plan to address the mental health concerns in our community. Mental health is and continues to be a major issue in communities across the country. The ongoing pandemic has added to the problem. It is a blessing to provide scholarships to local students who want to someday be mental health professionals, and perhaps return to Mesquite to aid us in our efforts on this issue.”

Mesquite Police Association Vice President Richard Andersen and Mesquite Firefighters Association-Local 1518 President Phil Cunningham presented the donation to Mesquite NAACP President Henry Brown during a ceremony in the office of Mayor Archer. Mr. Brown was joined by Mesquite NAACP board members Hosea Fain and Lloyd Jones.

Mayor Archer shared that the City’s comprehensive plan continues to be developed and will include training for public safety responders handling calls for service involving a mental health issue and the addition of staff with expertise in mental health. He also indicated the City plans to continue working with community partners to ensure a multifaceted approach is in place when responding to calls for service that require a mental health professional.

Dallas Regional Medical Center represented by Chief Executive Officer Glenda Matchett donated in September to support the new scholarship initiative for students, followed by a contribution in November from the USA-Nepal Community Outreach Network led by local physician Vishnu Upadhyay. In October, the City announced it would begin offering its residents access to free mental health counseling and referral services through a new partnership with the AZAR Foundation in which residents can call 833-298-9111 to schedule a virtual or in-person session for individuals, couples and groups.

Mayor Archer explained, “All through America, in every city and in every state, we see the great challenges that mental health can bring to every community with the lack of support, help and treatment. Here in Mesquite we take it very seriously. Our City Council is committed to doing more and more to help with the various issues that make-up the mental health crisis.”

Pain World - Temporada 1 (2025)

🎬 Sobre a Série:

Pain World é uma série animada independente criada por João Guilherme Brandão Neto em 2025.

Inspirada por animes como Neon Genesis Evangelion, misturada com a estética crua de cartoons amadores da internet, Pain World conta uma história emocional, filosófica e muitas vezes depressiva sobre amadurecimento, perda e esperança.

Apesar da animação simples e do orçamento praticamente inexistente, a série se destaca por seu roteiro emocional, personagens cativantes e um forte clima de "fim do mundo" em cada episódio.

📚 Sinopse:

Dan, um garoto comum, se vê envolvido em uma série de eventos catastróficos que vão muito além da compreensão humana.
Ao lado de seus amigos – incluindo o misterioso Shizuka – Dan precisa enfrentar batalhas internas, dilemas existenciais e até a destruição iminente da realidade como ele conhece.

Entre lágrimas, reflexões e episódios com animação limitada, Pain World mergulha o espectador em um mundo onde o que importa não é a qualidade visual, mas a carga emocional.

DragonBallDaimaAdultSwimBrasil_file30.desc:
Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

raton_esponja_latino_file0.desc:
Ratón esponja es una serie animada estadounidense, emitida inicialmente entre el 19 de julio de 2005 y el 18 de diciembre de 2006 en el bloque de programación Adult Swim de Cartoon Network. El programa fue creado y escrito por Matt Maiellaro y producido por Williams Street. El show se caracteriza por su cruda animación y sus bocetos simplificados, así como por su historia que mezcla suspenso, humor absurdo y thriller psicológico. En Latinoamérica, la serie fue transmitida por Cartoon Network desde el 2 de febrero de 2007 hasta el 7 de marzo de 2008, luego retransmitida por I.Sat desde 2008 hasta 2010 doblada al español, y desde 2015 hasta abril de 2020 subtitulada.

El show se centra en Ratón Fitzgerald o "Fitz", un ratón antropomórfico en busca de respuestas sobre su mundo y su pasado. La historia trata sobre una extraña conspiración, que se construye sobre sí misma, y que en ocasiones llega a ser torcida o no lineal, dejando pistas en cada episodio (incluso en los créditos), pero se va armando hasta develar una historia concreta y profunda.

If you thought that this movie is about cowboys killing illegal Mexican immigrant, you're both wrong and a complete racist. No, these cowboys are fighting against a much different kind of alien. You know the ones I'm talking about!

"Cowboys & Aliens" is a 2011 film adaptation of the graphic novel by Scott Mitchell Rosenberg. It was directed by Jon Favreau and starred Daniel Craig, Harrison Ford, Olivia Wilde, Sam Rockwell, Adam Beach, Paul Dano, and Noah Ringer.

The movie received mixed reviews from critics, who praised the acting performances and visual effects, but criticized the screenplay and tone, which played the comical premise straight. It was also a box office bomb, grossing $174 million against a $163 million budget.

A story of revenge and self-discovery, Shenmue debuted first as a video game by SEGA for the Dreamcast in 1999. scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.show: Scooby Doo & Scrappy Doo (1980 TV Series)
scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.desc:
Last Uploaded: January 30, 2022
Updated On: January 24, 2023



Significant changes were made to the show in its second season. Fred, Velma, and Daphne were dropped from the series and Shaggy, Scooby traveled all over the world on their own. Stories were also reduced to 7-minute segments and played at intervals with segments of mysteries but encountered geniune supernatural beings with Shaggy and Scooby did their best to escape and Scrappy did his best to splat. The tone of the stories was also changed to a typed of silly comedy, where the bad guys sometimes even talked directly to the camera. This format was used throughout the second and third seasons.

In the series' fourth and final season its format was changed again. Segments were not rotated with an unrelated canine cartoon starring Petey the Puppy as part of The Scooby & Scrappy-Doo/Puppy Hour. Stories reverted to the more traditional tone Scooby fans were used to except that, with a few exceptions, the supernatural element was eliminated from the show althougher. Shaggy, Scooby, and Scrappy worked for the Fearless Detective Agency, owned by Shaggy's uncle Fearless Shaggyfurt, on cases that involved ordinary criminals committing common crimes. Shaggy's uncle was never seen, but when he had a case, he would telephone the agency and give the three their assignment. At other times they took cases directly form visiting clients. How Shaggy and Scooby came to work for the agency may have been the biggest mystery, because they remained as cowardly as ever and were never enthusiastic about getting a case.

An additional segment was included in the fourth season that starred Scrappy-Doo and his (other) Uncle Yabba-Doo. Stories took place in the remote Western town of Tumbleweed, where Scrappy and Yabba assisted the town deputy, Dusty, in maintaing law and order.

As was customary with the Scooby-Doo cartoons, before long the show was reformatted and presented under a different title in order to keep things fresh. In 1983 a new series was produced called The New Scooby & Scrappy-Doo Show. Scrappy-Doo was also later featured in The New Scooby-Doo Mysteries (1984) and The 13 Ghosts of Scooby-Doo (1985). Other shows with a and Shaggy and Scooby-Doo Get a Clue! (2005).



Note 01: The last previous set of Scooby-Doo & Scrappy-Doo (1980 TV Series) had the shorts below 480p video image quality with some very old recordings as well. However, I was able to converge them by using the best quality films found in Czech and speed videos up a pinch to match each audio along with episodes. So I used the old recordings to copy and paste English audios into each video with better quality after muting Czech audios.

Note 02 (Update): 😊 A fellow patreon named Randy Miller has just resolved two videos of its features for Season 04! He was able to converge each segment into the best video quality (for 04-04B - Takeout Stakeout and 04-12 - Up a Crazy River) and then connect them together as one. Hurray!!

I have now inserted those two episdoes in full version, so please do download episodes 04-04 & 04-12 at the fullest version along with Season 04 (...and bypass the old segments as I tried to removed them and they didn't work). Also, full credits goes to Randy Miller (7randymiler@gmail.com) for finding the links with Google Drive and converging the videos into the best qualities for those two episdoes as well.



The email link you can contact me is louieseward@gmail.com. Many thanks, and enjoy!

DragonBallDaimaAdultSwimBrasil_file7.desc:
Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

the-oblongs-s-01-e-01-misfit-love_file16.desc:
The Oblongs is an American adult animated sitcom created by Angus Oblong and Jace Richdale. It was Mohawk Productions' first venture into animation. The series premiered on April 1, 2001, on The WB, and was cancelled due to low ratings on May 20, leaving the last five episodes unaired.[1] The remaining episodes were later aired on Cartoon Network's late-night programming block Adult Swim in August 2002, with the series premiering on the network in production order. The series is loosely based on a series of characters introduced in a picture book entitled Creepy Susie and 13 Other Tragic Tales for Troubled Children.[2]

Warner Bros. won the bidding for the rights to turn Angus Oblong's characters into a series; Fox and ABC also made unsuccessful bids. The show was produced by Film Roman, Oblong Productions, Jobsite Productions and Mohawk Productions in association with Warner Bros. Television, and the theme song for the series was composed and performed by They Might Be Giants. This was the only animated series to date to have been produced by Bruce Helford under Mohawk.

A total of 13 episodes were produced. All thirteen episodes of The Oblongs were released on DVD on October 4, 2005.

These are 480p DVD rips ran through Topaz AI several times for best quality results. the video was kept in the original 4:3 square format, & Upscaled to 1080p. Only 1080p copy I have seen on the internet right now at 9/20/25. Enjoy!

scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.show: Scooby Doo & Scrappy Doo (1980 TV Series)
scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.desc:
Last Uploaded: January 30, 2022
Updated On: January 24, 2023



Significant changes were made to the show in its second season. Fred, Velma, and Daphne were dropped from the series and Shaggy, Scooby traveled all over the world on their own. Stories were also reduced to 7-minute segments and played at intervals with segments of mysteries but encountered geniune supernatural beings with Shaggy and Scooby did their best to escape and Scrappy did his best to splat. The tone of the stories was also changed to a typed of silly comedy, where the bad guys sometimes even talked directly to the camera. This format was used throughout the second and third seasons.

In the series' fourth and final season its format was changed again. Segments were not rotated with an unrelated canine cartoon starring Petey the Puppy as part of The Scooby & Scrappy-Doo/Puppy Hour. Stories reverted to the more traditional tone Scooby fans were used to except that, with a few exceptions, the supernatural element was eliminated from the show althougher. Shaggy, Scooby, and Scrappy worked for the Fearless Detective Agency, owned by Shaggy's uncle Fearless Shaggyfurt, on cases that involved ordinary criminals committing common crimes. Shaggy's uncle was never seen, but when he had a case, he would telephone the agency and give the three their assignment. At other times they took cases directly form visiting clients. How Shaggy and Scooby came to work for the agency may have been the biggest mystery, because they remained as cowardly as ever and were never enthusiastic about getting a case.

An additional segment was included in the fourth season that starred Scrappy-Doo and his (other) Uncle Yabba-Doo. Stories took place in the remote Western town of Tumbleweed, where Scrappy and Yabba assisted the town deputy, Dusty, in maintaing law and order.

As was customary with the Scooby-Doo cartoons, before long the show was reformatted and presented under a different title in order to keep things fresh. In 1983 a new series was produced called The New Scooby & Scrappy-Doo Show. Scrappy-Doo was also later featured in The New Scooby-Doo Mysteries (1984) and The 13 Ghosts of Scooby-Doo (1985). Other shows with a and Shaggy and Scooby-Doo Get a Clue! (2005).



Note 01: The last previous set of Scooby-Doo & Scrappy-Doo (1980 TV Series) had the shorts below 480p video image quality with some very old recordings as well. However, I was able to converge them by using the best quality films found in Czech and speed videos up a pinch to match each audio along with episodes. So I used the old recordings to copy and paste English audios into each video with better quality after muting Czech audios.

Note 02 (Update): 😊 A fellow patreon named Randy Miller has just resolved two videos of its features for Season 04! He was able to converge each segment into the best video quality (for 04-04B - Takeout Stakeout and 04-12 - Up a Crazy River) and then connect them together as one. Hurray!!

I have now inserted those two episdoes in full version, so please do download episodes 04-04 & 04-12 at the fullest version along with Season 04 (...and bypass the old segments as I tried to removed them and they didn't work). Also, full credits goes to Randy Miller (7randymiler@gmail.com) for finding the links with Google Drive and converging the videos into the best qualities for those two episdoes as well.



The email link you can contact me is louieseward@gmail.com. Many thanks, and enjoy!

DragonBallDaimaAdultSwimBrasil_file11.desc:
Sinopse:
Em Dragon Ball Daima, uma misteriosa conspiração abala o universo: Goku, Vegeta, Bulma e outros heróis são transformados em crianças por forças desconhecidas que parecem ter ligação com o Outro Mundo. Sem a força total que possuíam em suas formas adultas, eles precisam embarcar em uma nova jornada através de mundos inexplorados, enfrentando inimigos perigosos e descobrindo a origem desse estranho feitiço. Agora, com o bastão mágico e a Nuvem Voadora de volta, Goku e seus amigos terão que confiar mais em sua inteligência, coragem e trabalho em equipe do que nunca. Em meio a combates intensos e aventuras cheias de humor e emoção, Daima resgata o espírito clássico de Dragon Ball, enquanto revela uma ameaça que pode ser ainda maior do que qualquer outra que eles já enfrentaram.

Produção:
Dragon Ball Daima é o novo anime da franquia Dragon Ball, criado em comemoração aos 40 anos da obra original de Akira Toriyama. A série está sendo produzida pela Toei Animation, com estreia acontecendo em outubro de 2024. A estreia mundial aconteceu em outubro de 2024, com exibição simultânea em diversos países, incluindo Japão, Estados Unidos e Brasil pela plataforma de streaming 'Crunchyroll'.

No Brasil, Dragon Ball Daima teve sua primeira exibição oficial dublada em fevereiro de 2025, quando os três episódios iniciais foram lançados nos cinemas em formato de filme especial, oferecendo aos fãs uma prévia exclusiva da nova fase da franquia. A recepção foi positiva, especialmente pela qualidade da animação e pela nostalgia evocada pelo retorno de Goku e seus amigos em versões infantis. Posteriormente, a série chegou à mídia tradicional no dia 25 de abril de 2025, sendo transmitida pelo canal Adult Swim, conhecido por exibir animações mais voltadas ao público jovem e adulto e pertencente ao mesmo grupo do Cartoon Network. A dublagem brasileira manteve o elenco clássico, e a estreia na televisão trouxe também uma nova leva de fãs que aguardavam acompanhar semanalmente o desenrolar da história.

scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.show: Scooby Doo & Scrappy Doo (1980 TV Series)
scooby-doo-and-scrappy-doo-02-1980-tv-se.desc:
Last Uploaded: January 30, 2022
Updated On: January 24, 2023



Significant changes were made to the show in its second season. Fred, Velma, and Daphne were dropped from the series and Shaggy, Scooby traveled all over the world on their own. Stories were also reduced to 7-minute segments and played at intervals with segments of mysteries but encountered geniune supernatural beings with Shaggy and Scooby did their best to escape and Scrappy did his best to splat. The tone of the stories was also changed to a typed of silly comedy, where the bad guys sometimes even talked directly to the camera. This format was used throughout the second and third seasons.

In the series' fourth and final season its format was changed again. Segments were not rotated with an unrelated canine cartoon starring Petey the Puppy as part of The Scooby & Scrappy-Doo/Puppy Hour. Stories reverted to the more traditional tone Scooby fans were used to except that, with a few exceptions, the supernatural element was eliminated from the show althougher. Shaggy, Scooby, and Scrappy worked for the Fearless Detective Agency, owned by Shaggy's uncle Fearless Shaggyfurt, on cases that involved ordinary criminals committing common crimes. Shaggy's uncle was never seen, but when he had a case, he would telephone the agency and give the three their assignment. At other times they took cases directly form visiting clients. How Shaggy and Scooby came to work for the agency may have been the biggest mystery, because they remained as cowardly as ever and were never enthusiastic about getting a case.

An additional segment was included in the fourth season that starred Scrappy-Doo and his (other) Uncle Yabba-Doo. Stories took place in the remote Western town of Tumbleweed, where Scrappy and Yabba assisted the town deputy, Dusty, in maintaing law and order.

As was customary with the Scooby-Doo cartoons, before long the show was reformatted and presented under a different title in order to keep things fresh. In 1983 a new series was produced called The New Scooby & Scrappy-Doo Show. Scrappy-Doo was also later featured in The New Scooby-Doo Mysteries (1984) and The 13 Ghosts of Scooby-Doo (1985). Other shows with a and Shaggy and Scooby-Doo Get a Clue! (2005).



Note 01: The last previous set of Scooby-Doo & Scrappy-Doo (1980 TV Series) had the shorts below 480p video image quality with some very old recordings as well. However, I was able to converge them by using the best quality films found in Czech and speed videos up a pinch to match each audio along with episodes. So I used the old recordings to copy and paste English audios into each video with better quality after muting Czech audios.

Note 02 (Update): 😊 A fellow patreon named Randy Miller has just resolved two videos of its features for Season 04! He was able to converge each segment into the best video quality (for 04-04B - Takeout Stakeout and 04-12 - Up a Crazy River) and then connect them together as one. Hurray!!

I have now inserted those two episdoes in full version, so please do download episodes 04-04 & 04-12 at the fullest version along with Season 04 (...and bypass the old segments as I tried to removed them and they didn't work). Also, full credits goes to Randy Miller (7randymiler@gmail.com) for finding the links with Google Drive and converging the videos into the best qualities for those two episdoes as well.



The email link you can contact me is louieseward@gmail.com. Many thanks, and enjoy!

opening-to-monster-hunter-2021-dvd_file1.desc:
After the "Resident Evil" film series concluded its original continuity, Paul W.S. Anderson decided to adapt another popular Capcom property.

"Monster Hunter" is a 2020 film adaptation of the video game franchise of the same name. It was written, produced, and directed by Paul W.S. Anderson and starred Milla Jovovich, Tony Jaa, Tip "T.I." Harris, Meagan Good, Diego Boneta, Josh Helman, Jin Au-Yeung, and Ron Perlman.

This movie was first concepted in 2012, but filming didn't start until October 2018, when Capcom first announced it.

The movie received mixed reviews from critics, who praised the action sequences, visual effects, and Paul Haslinger's music score, but criticized Anderson's directing and editing.

It also gained backlash in China due to some dialogue in which Axe (played by Jin Au-Yeung) refers to his knees as "Chi-knees", a reference to a racist stereotype, resulting in the movie being temporarily pulled from Chinese theaters in order to edit out the offending dialogue. This also resulted in its U.S. release to be delayed as well.

Worse still, the movie was also a box office bomb, due to having a limited theatrical run due to the COVID-19 pandemic. It grossed $44 million on a $60 million budget. Despite its commercial failure, both Paul W.S. Anderson and Milla Jovovich are thinking about doing a sequel together.

CRITICAL RESPONSE:
Rotten Tomatoes - 44%
Metacritic - 47%

The opening credits to this movie have been edited to avoid copyright issues.

Please do not give me any requests for videos. I make these posts out of the home media I pick at random.

"Monster Hunter" (2020) is a property of Screen Gems, Constantin Film, Tencent Pictures, Toho, AB2 Digital Pictures, and Capcom. All other properties belong to their respective owners.

deep-dive-on-evangelion_file54.desc:
This is a compilation of now-deleted videos from a Korean YouTuber who created very deep analyses of Evangelion.

According to the YouTuber’s own account, they first deleted the videos after receiving a warning from Studio Khara. They also said that Khara continued to pressure them and threatened legal action even after the videos were taken down.

nostalgiaetherealcollection_file13.desc:
Welcome to nostalgia central. NostalgiaEthereal's aka The Retro Connoisseur's wide collection of nostalgic content all in one place for everyone caring to go back in time for hours upon hours. VHS recordings ranging from the 90's to 00's, long marathons of obscure television, vintage cartoons and anime, retro drive-in movie projects, TV-movie broadcast recreations, original commercials throughout, Adult Swim-related content, and September 11th-related content. This is a time-machine in the form of a database. As time progresses, more will be added as I acquire more VHS tapes to transfer. So be on the lookout as time moves on for new content uploaded. Archiving these things is HIGHLY important and crucial to preservation of a time period that has long past and must be kept alive through data-hoarding archived media catalogues such as this.
I do not own any of the content shared here. Only for historical preservation and entertainment purposes.

the-boondocks-uncensored-dvd_file59.desc: The Boondocks is an American adult animated black sitcom, created by Aaron McGruder for Cartoon Network's late-night programming block, Adult Swim. It is based upon his manga-influenced comic strip of the same name. The series premiered on November 6, 2005. The show focuses on a Black American family, the Freemans, settling into the fictional, friendly and predominantly White suburb of Woodcrest. The perspective offered by this mixture of cultures, lifestyles, social classes, stereotypes, viewpoints and racialized identities provides for much of the series' satire, comedy, and conflict.
